evidence: we are integrating artificial intelligence to support our internal business functions and exploring additional uses for the future
In recent years, we have increased our investment in technology and automation enabling us to operate more efficiently
As part of our broader digital transformation strategy, we are integrating artificial intelligence to support our internal business functions and exploring additional uses for the future
we aim to develop and use AI responsibly
AI algorithms and training methodologies may be flawed... AI-related issues, deficiencies and failures could also give rise to legal or regulatory action
Our obligations to comply with the evolving legal and regulatory landscape could entail significant costs or limit our ability to incorporate certain AI capabilities
the increasing use of machine learning, artificial intelligence (AI) and other sophisticated techniques to initiate cyber attacks and social engineering threats, such as phishing or deepfake schemes

limitations: ai_investment_intensity: No dollar amounts or quantified AI-specific spend disclosed; general technology capex (~1% of net sales) is not attributed to AI specifically
ai_narrative_concreteness: AI use described only in aspirational/exploratory terms with no named initiatives, deployed systems, or quantified outcomes; promotional language noted

ai_risk_disclosure_depth: Risk-section material is present; scored 4 because specific risk mechanisms are described (regulatory compliance costs, legal liability, operational failures, jurisdiction-specific legislation) though no quantification or named governance body specifically for AI risk beyond the general Audit Committee oversight
